name: Herencia from Villarta de San Juan – circular by Puerto Lápice
name_original: Herencia desde Villarta de San Juan – circular por Puerto Lápice
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villarta De San Juan traverse the flat plains of La Mancha, a region characterized by its agricultural landscapes and historic windmill towns. The terrain is generally level, with gentle undulations, making it suitable for sustained road cycling efforts. Routes often follow quiet country roads, passing through open fields and alongside the occasional river, such as the Rio Cigüela. Best road cycling routes around Villarta De San Juan The most popular road cycling route is Herencia from Villarta de San Juan – circular by Puerto Lápice, a 36.9 miles (59.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tour of La Mancha's Windmill Towns, a difficult 90.3 miles (145.3 km) path. This route provides an extensive exploration of the region's iconic windmills and expansive plains. Local road cyclists also love the Ermita de San Antón – Herencia loop from Alcázar de San Juan, a 52.2 miles (84.1 km) trail leading through agricultural land and small towns, often completed in about 4 hours 45 minutes. Road cycling around Villarta De San Juan is defined by flat plains, agricultural fields, and historic windmill towns. The network offers options for various ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Villarta De San Juan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 60 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villarta De San Juan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villarta De San Juan?
A: There are 7 road cycling routes around Villarta De San Juan, offering a variety of options for different skill levels.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling in Villarta De San Juan?
A: The routes around Villarta De San Juan are predominantly moderate, with 5 moderate routes, 1 easy route, and 1 difficult route. The terrain is generally flat with gentle undulations, characteristic of the La Mancha plains.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for beginners, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Antón – Herencia loop from Herencia&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route, covering approximately 44.9 km with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of distances can I expect on road cycling routes near Villarta De San Juan?
A: Routes vary in length, from shorter rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old water well – Rio Cigüela loop from Villarta de San Juan&lt;/a&gt; at about 46.6 km, to longer, more challenging tours such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080733093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of La Mancha's Windmill Towns&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 145 km.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079488682/herencia-from-villarta-de-san-juan-circular-by-puerto-lapice" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herencia from Villarta de San Juan – circular by Puerto Lápice&lt;/a&gt;, a 59.5 km loop that takes about 2 hours 33 minutes.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes around Villarta De San Juan?
A: The routes often pass through agricultural landscapes and historic windmill towns. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5020745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Lápice Windmills&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also encounter the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4382300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of La Mancha&lt;/a&gt; on some longer tours.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villarta De San Juan?
A: The region experiences hot, dry summers and mild winters. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense summer heat.

Q: What are the typical weather conditions for road cycling in Villarta De San Juan?
A: Villarta De San Juan is located in La Mancha, known for its continental climate with hot, dry summers and cold winters. Expect clear skies and significant temperature variations between day and night, especially outside of summer. Wind can also be a factor across the open plains.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Villarta De San Juan?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often appreciate the flat plains and quiet country roads that allow for sustained efforts, as well as the unique experience of passing through historic windmill towns.

Q: Are there any advanced or challenging road cycling routes?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080733093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of La Mancha's Windmill Towns&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route, spanning over 145 km and providing an extensive exploration of the region's iconic landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by the Rio Cigüela?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old water well – Rio Cigüela loop from Villarta de San Juan&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that follows the Rio Cigüela, offering views of the river and surrounding agricultural land.

Q: Are the road cycling routes suitable for families?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not explicitly highlighted, the generally flat terrain and quiet country roads make some of the easier, shorter routes potentially suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with longer distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Antón – Herencia loop from Herencia&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option to consider.
tips:
In a place in La Mancha lies Puerto Lápice, whose name the follower of Don Quixote's steps will remember for its evocation of him, because everything that surrounds it evokes the masterful novel that mentions this town up to four times. Its name also remembers its meeting of paths in the foothills of the Montes de Toledo and the lapice stone ground that we walk on. “And, speaking about the last adventure, they continued on their way to Puerto Lápice, because there Don Quixote said that it was impossible not to encounter many and diverse adventures, because it was a very temporary place.”

The whitewashed houses and their typically La Mancha construction do not undo the literary spell. The Plaza Mayor is a construction with two floors of arcades, supported by wooden footings and uprights, painted in the characteristic reddish color of La Mancha. Details of the Ingenious Hidalgo are hidden everywhere.

The sales that are the origin of the population maintain their structure and some revive in their patios, in their activity and even in their type of service, meetings, appointments or memories of Cervantes' masterful novel.

Herencia owes its creation to the Order of San Juan, during the Reconquest, but its origin dates back centuries, as evidenced by the remains of forts found in the Sierra de los Molinos and Pico del Navajo, from the Bronze Age. In addition to its industrial heritage, Herencia is land of agricultural production, two churches stand out in this municipality: the parish church and the one of the Convent of La Merced, these will be the nucleus of the visit to this town of La Mancha.
Short and steep climb to the communication antennas. Incredible views of the surrounding landscape.
